Bombardier Q1 2026 results, April 30, 2026

Limited public data

Engine supply disruptions causing production stoppages

Weeks of downtime

One unidentified engine OEM caused brutal supply problems in 2025 that stopped production lines for weeks.

Capabilities Needed

Supplier Risk ManagementMulti-Tier VisibilityAlternative Sourcing Strategy

Q1 2026 supplier snags delayed deliveries

24 deliveries vs plan

Temporary supplier snags slowed operations and delayed Q1 2026 deliveries though now resolved.

Real-Time Supply VisibilityDemand-Supply MatchingDelivery Promise Management

Supply chain costs compressed margins

-90bps EBITDA margin

Adjusted EBITDA margin contracted from 16.3% to 15.4% in Q1 2026 due to transitory supply chain costs.

Capabilities Needed

Cost-to-Serve OptimizationMargin AnalyticsScenario Planning
WC: $250mInv: -8%Margin: +150bps

Where the value lies

Bombardier's $20.3bn backlog and 3.6x book-to-bill create urgent need to eliminate supplier disruptions that cost weeks of production downtime and compressed margins by 90bps in Q1 2026. With >$10bn revenue target and free cash flow guidance raised to >$1bn, stabilizing supply chain can unlock $250m working capital and recover 150bps margin lost to transitory supply costs.

Planning Opportunities

With $2.06bn revenue and 70% of production capacity currently blocked by Chinese export controls, Nexperia faces $1.4bn in stranded revenue requiring immediate supply chain reconfiguration. By implementing integrated planning across remaining European facilities (Hamburg, Manchester) and alternative Asian sites (Malaysia, Philippines), Nexperia can release $180m in working capital through inventory rationalization, improve gross margin by 250bps through network optimization, and restore customer OTIF from current failure state to 85%+ within 6 months.
